parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: When enabled, will actively update the fixture and universe data, and if used in a DMX Out will be sent out to the device. If disabled, the DMX Out will not merge the DMX universes constructed by this

parameters.name: autolayout
parameters.label: Auto Layout
parameters.description: When enabled, the universes will be constructed sequentially starting from net, subnet, universe, and channel specified in the parameters. For example, if starting a net=0, subnet=0, universe=1, chann

parameters.name: routingtable
parameters.label: Routing Table
parameters.description: Use a Routing Table to assign specific net, subnet, universe values to a fixture/primitive. Additionally, netaddress may optionally be specified to override those on the DMX Out . The routing table wi

parameters.name: net
parameters.label: Net (0-127)
parameters.description: When using Auto , specify the starting net value.

parameters.name: subnet
parameters.label: Subnet (0-15)
parameters.description: When using Auto , specify the starting subnet value.

parameters.name: universe
parameters.label: Universe
parameters.description: When using Auto , specify the starting universe value.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.label: Channel
parameters.description: When using Auto , specify the starting channel value.

parameters.name: changap
parameters.label: Channel Gap
parameters.description: When using Auto , specify the amount of channels between fixtures. Eg. if fixture 1 ends on channel 200 and there is a channel of 10, then fixture 2 will start on channel 211.

parameters.name: quantizeuni
parameters.label: Quantize Universe
parameters.description: Specify the level of quantizing to perform within a universe. It can either be set to be done By Fixture, or By .

parameters.name: dmxchan
parameters.label: DMX Channel
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks used to define the DMX profile

parameters.name: dmxchan0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: Specify the unique DMX Channel identifier.

parameters.name: dmxchan0valuetype
parameters.label: Value Type
parameters.description: Specify how the channels values are iterated, and how the attributes are sourced.

parameters.name: dmxchan0valuesource
parameters.label: Value Source
parameters.description: For the DMX channel value, gets it from an attribute or from a parameter.

parameters.name: dmxchan0group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: Optionally select a point group. Only points within the group will be used for universe creation, rather than all points.

parameters.name: dmxchan0numcomps
parameters.label: Number of Components
parameters.description: Number of components of the new attribute.

parameters.name: dmxchan0attr
parameters.label: Attribute
parameters.description: Set the attributes to interleave. Multiple attributes can be specified together.

parameters.name: dmxchan0valueres
parameters.label: Value Resolution
parameters.description: The value resolution. Note: one DMX channel is 8-bit (ie. one byte and 0-255), so if a value is greater than 8-bit, it will use more than one channel: Eg. 16-bit uses 2 channels.

parameters.name: dmxchan0normalized
parameters.label: Value is Normalized
parameters.description: When enabled, values of 0-1 are expected, and they will be scaled to the appropriate range for the resolution (ie. 0-255 for 8-bit). When disabled, the values will be output as-is. Clamping is done in

parameters.name: dmxchan0merge
parameters.label: Merge with Above Block
parameters.description: When enabled, will interleave channel values with the above block. The blocks must be of the same value type and value source. This feature is useful if needing to interleave values of different resol

parameters.name: dmxchan0interleavebytes
parameters.label: Interleave Bytes
parameters.description: When enabled, attribute component values will be interleaved by byte order, instead of value order. Example: Position attribute P with 16-bit resolution will be interleaved as when the toggle is disab

parameters.name: bypass
parameters.label: Bypass
parameters.description: Pass through the first input to the output unchanged.

parameters.name: freeextragpumem
parameters.label: Free Extra GPU Memory
parameters.description: Free memory that has accumulated when output memory has grown and shrunk.

parameters.name: delinputattrs
parameters.label: Delete Input Attributes
parameters.description: Only output which attributes you specify in this helps isolate attributes into a separate branch.

parameters.name: dmxchan0value3
parameters.label: Dmxchan0Value3
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

examples.label: Configure Name and Attribute
examples.description: Common settings from 11 real Derivative examples

examples.label: Alternative configuration
examples.code:
n = op('dmxfixture1')
n.par.dmxchan0name = 'RGB'
n.par.dmxchan0attr = 'Color(0) Color(1) Color(2)'
